\noindent To test the asumption of constant variance, we can employ visual means, such as the \ttblue{residualPlots} in Figure 5.15. We can construct it as follows.

\par\addvspace{\topsep}
\begingroup
\fvset{listparameters={%
  \setlength{\topsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\partopsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\parsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\itemsep}{0pt}%
}}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_blue}]
residualPlots(ussteamco.mod.3, layout = c(2, 3),
              quadratic = FALSE, linear = TRUE)
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_light_blue}]
           Test stat Pr(>|Test stat|)
production   -0.8556           0.3997
heatDD       -1.2257           0.2309
coolDD       -0.3821           0.7054
summer        0.0487           0.9615
Tukey test   -0.8414           0.4001
\end{Verbatim}
\endgroup
\par\addvspace{\topsep}
\noindent The formal statistical test is the Breusch-Pagan test.
